From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani singer and actor

| Uzair Jaswal | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | (1992-11-22) 22 November 1992 (age 33)  [Oregon](/wiki/Oregon "Oregon"), United States |
| Occupations | * Singer-songwriter * model * actor |
| Years active | 2009–present |
| Relatives | * [Umair Jaswal](/wiki/Umair_Jaswal "Umair Jaswal") (brother) * [Yasir Jaswal](/wiki/Yasir_Jaswal "Yasir Jaswal") (brother) |
| **Musical career** | |
| Genres | [Pop](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music") |
| Instruments | * Vocals * Guitar |
| Years active | 2009–present |
| Musical artist | |

**Uzair Jaswal** ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): عذیرجسوال) is a [Pakistani](/wiki/Pakistani "Pakistani") singer-songwriter and actor.[[1]](#cite_note-auto1-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Uzair_Jaswal&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f1/Momina_Mustehsan_and_Uzair_Jaswal_at_New_Islamabad_Airport.jpg/250px-Momina_Mustehsan_and_Uzair_Jaswal_at_New_Islamabad_Airport.jpg)](/wiki/File:Momina_Mustehsan_and_Uzair_Jaswal_at_New_Islamabad_Airport.jpg)

Momina Mustehsan and Uzair Jaswal

Uzair started his career at the age of 14.[[2]](#cite_note-2) At 16, he wrote his first song and was the lead singer of a band called Scar which performed alternative-rock covers.[[3]](#cite_note-auto-3) Some of his popular singles include "Tere Bin", "Jaanvey", "Meree Rooh", "Bolay" and "Yaheen". His tracks "Nindiya Ke Paar" and "Bolay" were featured in the 5th season of *Coke Studio*.[[4]](#cite_note-4) At 21, he was the youngest performer of [*Coke Studio* Pakistan](/wiki/Coke_Studio_(Pakistan) "Coke Studio (Pakistan)") in its first 7 seasons.[[3]](#cite_note-auto-3)[[1]](#cite_note-auto1-1)

Uzair branched out into acting. He has worked in three Pakistani films, namely, [Gol Chakkar](/wiki/Gol_Chakkar "Gol Chakkar"),[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) [Slackistan](/wiki/Slackistan "Slackistan")[[7]](#cite_note-7) and [Jalaibee](/wiki/Jalaibee "Jalaibee").[[8]](#cite_note-8) He debuted on Pakistani TV with the drama series *Moray Saiyaan* on [ARY Digital](/wiki/ARY_Digital "ARY Digital") in 2016.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

He released his debut album titled "Na Bhulana" in the end of 2016, after a decade of making music. The album contains 11 individual tracks and is titled on the hit song "Na Bhulana" which was also the title track of his drama *[Moray Saiyaan](/w/index.php?title=Moray_Saiyaan&action=edit&redlink=1 "Moray Saiyaan (page does not exist)").*[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) He won the award for the "Album of The Year" at the [16th Lux Style Awards](/wiki/16th_Lux_Style_Awards "16th Lux Style Awards") for "Na Bhulana".[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)
His drama *[Shayad](/wiki/Shayad "Shayad")* is currently airing on [Geo Entertainment](/wiki/Geo_Entertainment "Geo Entertainment") where he is performing impressively.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Bollywood Debut

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Uzair_Jaswal&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Bollywood Debut")]

Jaswal made his playback singing debut in the [Bollywood](/wiki/Bollywood "Bollywood") film *[Ek Paheli Leela](/wiki/Ek_Paheli_Leela "Ek Paheli Leela")* in 2015. He sang the song titled "Tere Bin Nahi Laage", which was a recreated version of his earlier hit, "Tere Bin". The song was recreated by [Amaal Mallik](/wiki/Amaal_Mallik "Amaal Mallik") with a few lyrics added by [Kumaar](/wiki/Kumaar "Kumaar") for the film.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)
"Tere Bin" won The Gold Disc Award at the Radio Mirchi Awards in India.yes[[18]](#cite_note-18)
